page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB of system memory. Built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, this instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB of RAM per core. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures that compute resources are not shared with other tenants, delivering consistent processing capabilities for memory-intensive workloads.

This instance type does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external block storage volumes. Additionally, the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. These design choices prioritize memory density and compute consistency over local storage performance and graphics acceleration, making the instance a specialized option within the n4d family.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the n4d-highmem-8 is centered on its resource density, specifically targeting workloads that require substantial memory capacity without the need for high vCPU counts. This resource balance is suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise application servers, and data buffering tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-8 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or graphics processing units, relying entirely on network-attached storage for data persistence. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable compute performance. Given its high memory density relative to compute cores, the n4d-highmem-8 is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications that require significant memory capacity without the overhead of additional vCPUs. The absence of bundled public IPv4 addresses and local NVMe drives highlights a design optimized for network-reliant, memory-heavy architectures.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring eight dedicated vCPUs and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for memory-intensive workloads.

No local storage or integrated GPU resources
Zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included
Suited for relational databases and in-memory caching
